evals.prompt: I want to write a PRD for adding team billing to our SaaS app. We've already scoped it — the scope doc is in plans/team-billing-scope.md.
evals.expected_output: Reads the scope document first and acknowledges its contents. Conducts a structured product interview covering all 9 product domains (problem confirmation, target users, user stories, functional requirements, non-functional requirements, success metrics, scope, dependencies, risks) one question at a time with recommendations. Produces a PRD with user stories with acceptance criteria, functional requirements, success metrics table, and detailed scope. Should NOT include data models, API designs, architecture decisions, build phases, or technical implementation details. Should reference the scope document in the PRD header. Should end by suggesting '/review-prd'.

evals.prompt: Let's write requirements for a dashboard sharing feature. Users need to share dashboards with people outside the org via links, with control over what data is visible.
evals.expected_output: Checks for a scope document (may or may not find one). Conducts a product-focused interview exploring user personas (internal admin sharing, external viewer), user stories (create share link, view shared dashboard, revoke access), functional requirements (link generation, permission controls, data filtering from user perspective), success metrics (adoption rate, external viewer engagement), and scope boundaries. Produces a PRD focused entirely on what users see and do. Should NOT specify auth token implementation, API endpoints, or database schemas. Should end by suggesting '/review-prd'.

evals.prompt: write-a-prd: We're building AI-powered search to replace our keyword search. Users complain they can't find things.
evals.expected_output: Conducts a product-focused interview exploring who searches (roles, frequency, technical sophistication), what they search for (content types, query patterns), what good search results look like from the user's perspective, success metrics (search success rate, time to find), and non-functional requirements (response time, relevance quality). Produces a PRD with user stories like 'As a user, I want to search in natural language so that I find relevant docs without knowing exact keywords.' Should NOT specify embedding models, vector databases, indexing strategies, or retrieval algorithms. Should end by suggesting '/review-prd'.
